memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to kill time, but my term project is barely started and due Thursday. I should probably focus on that instead of leisure or quick chores.
My energy is dipping, but ignoring that looming term project deadline would be a terrible trade-off for any leisure time right now.
With my term project due Thursday and barely started, I can't afford to waste this free afternoon on leisure or chores. I need to force myself into deep work now before my energy completely crashes.

It's 14:30 and the afternoon is slipping away while my project remains untouched. I can't afford this procrastination anymore; I need to force myself into deep work before my energy crashes completely.

desires: Finish a tangible chunk of the term project code/docs before my energy crashes completely, proving to myself I'm not wasting this afternoon.
Get that quick chore done instantly so I can stop feeling like I'm accumulating trivial distractions while the big task looms.
Avoid looking unreliable to my cohort if they ask for updates, even though I don't want to socialize right now.

goals.description: Execute the 'quick_chore' immediately to clear mental clutter, then dive into a deep work block on the term project before fatigue sets in.
goals.rationale: My procrastination bias is maxed out (7/10) and I'm already running on sleep debt. Doing the trivial task first gives me an instant dopamine hit to break the inertia, but I can't afford to let the term project slide again. The leisure option is a trap; my achievement drive demands I secure that 'A' now, not later.

agent.last_reasoning: I need that instant dopamine hit to break my inertia before the term project eats me alive. Skipping this feels like wasting a guaranteed win, but I won't linger; it's just a gateway to deep work now.
